name: conductor-status
description: Display project status, active tracks, and next actions
capabilities: Display overall project progress with percentage completion
Show individual track status (pending, in-progress, complete)
Identify current focus and next actions for each track
Detect and display blockers between dependent tracks
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 61 potential issues (external_commands, weak crypto, system reconnaissance) but manual review confirms these are false positives. The skill is a legitimate project management tool that reads local markdown and JSON files to display project status. No actual command execution, cryptographic operations, or network requests are performed.
valueStatement: Conductor-status displays project progress, active tracks, and next actions in a formatted dashboard. It helps teams stay organized by tracking tasks across multiple work streams.
limitations: Requires Conductor to be initialized with conductor:setup
Only reads local files in conductor/ directory structure
Cannot create or modify tracks (use other Conductor skills)
Does not integrate with external project management tools

useCases.title: Daily Standup Updates
useCases.description: Quickly review project progress and identify blockers before team meetings

useCases.title: Progress Reporting
useCases.description: Generate formatted status reports for stakeholders with detailed track breakdowns

useCases.title: Workflow Navigation
useCases.description: Identify the next task to work on based on current track phases and priorities

promptTemplates.title: Quick Status Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Use conductor-status to show the current project status. What is the overall progress percentage and which track is currently active?
promptTemplates.scenario: Get a rapid overview of project health

promptTemplates.title: Detailed Track Status
promptTemplates.prompt: Run conductor-status with the track ID [track-id] to see the detailed status of a specific track including its phase progress and next steps.
promptTemplates.scenario: Review a specific track in detail

promptTemplates.title: Identify Blockers
promptTemplates.prompt: Use conductor-status to check if there are any blockers in the project. What tracks are blocked and what are they waiting on?

promptTemplates.title: JSON Export
promptTemplates.prompt: Run conductor-status --json to get the full project status in JSON format for integration with other tools.

bestPractices: Run conductor-status before starting new work to understand current project state
Use --quick mode for brief updates in team communications
Check for blockers regularly to avoid wasted effort on blocked tasks
antiPatterns: Running conductor-status without first initializing Conductor with conductor:setup
Expecting external tool integration when this skill only reads local files
Using conductor-status to modify tasks (this skill is read-only)

faq.question: What is Conductor?
faq.answer: Conductor is a project management framework that organizes work into tracks. Each track contains phases and tasks stored as local markdown files.

faq.question: How do I initialize Conductor?
faq.answer: Run /conductor:setup to create the initial conductor/ directory structure with product.md and tracks.md files.

faq.question: Can this skill modify tasks?
faq.answer: No, conductor-status is read-only. Use conductor:implement or other Conductor skills to modify task status.

faq.question: What do the status markers mean?
How do I see a specific track?
faq.answer: Pass the track ID as an argument: conductor-status [track-id]

faq.question: Can I export the status data?
faq.answer: Yes, use --json flag to get machine-readable output for integration with other tools.
